faq.a: Both solve durable execution. Upstash Workflow is tighter with the Upstash ecosystem; Inngest is more event-driven.

name: Trigger.dev
agent_summary: Trigger.dev is the open-source background jobs platform for Next.js.
description: Run long-running background jobs with full reliability.

faq.a: Background jobs in Next.js and Node.js — tasks that take longer than a serverless function timeout allows.
Yes, Trigger.dev is fully open-source and can be self-hosted.
Yes, automatic retries with configurable backoff are built-in.

faq.q: How does Trigger.dev differ from a simple cron job?
faq.a: Trigger.dev jobs are event-driven, resumable, observable, and have built-in queuing — not just scheduled scripts.

name: Inngest
agent_summary: Inngest is the event-driven durable execution platform.
description: Durable and reliable background jobs triggered by events.

faq.a: Inngest functions are triggered by typed events — you send an event, Inngest queues and runs the matching functions reliably.
Yes, you can call step.sleep() inside a function and Inngest pauses execution — no infrastructure needed.
Yes, a generous free plan covering thousands of function runs per month.
Yes, Inngest is purpose-built for Vercel and Next.js — runs as a serverless API route.
